# External design pilot recruitment: three owner-consented slots

design-ai is recruiting project owners for three bounded external pilots:

1. one marketing or landing page;
2. one observable web or app workflow;
3. one Korean commerce or fintech interface.

Each pilot follows the public review-to-verified-iteration workflow: source-linked
review, separate owner consent, approved implementation scope, runtime and
accessibility evidence, and an exact before/after comparison.

## Apply

Use the [External design pilot Issue Form](https://github.com/sungjin9288/design-ai/issues/new?template=external-pilot.yml).

Read the [program guide](https://sungjin9288.github.io/design-ai/docs/EXTERNAL-PILOT-PROGRAM/)
before applying. It defines eligibility, data boundaries, measurement, stop
conditions, and the distinction between intake and consent.
The [operations guide](https://sungjin9288.github.io/design-ai/docs/EXTERNAL-PILOT-OPERATIONS/)
defines candidate status, the marketing-page first slot, browser evidence, and
the repeated-problem product decision gate.

## Public issue boundary

Do not post secrets, credentials, personal or customer data, private source,
analytics exports, local paths, or private preview links. A public repository or
page URL is optional. Private project details stay outside GitHub.

Submitting the form is candidate intake only. It does not authorize source
inspection, evidence collection, target mutation, dependency changes, migrations,
commit, push, deployment, or external writes. A separate owner-consent record is
required before any pilot begins.

## Evidence boundary

- Available slots: 3
- Identified participants at publication: 0
- Consented targets at publication: 0
- Completed external pilots at publication: 0
- External adoption claim: unverified

The next product capability will not be selected from applications or one anecdote.
The same user problem must appear in at least two completed, independent pilot
records from distinct project owners.
